reason: Surface is already Latin; Taiwanese always say 'Visa' for the payment network, never a Chinese transliteration.
愛馬仕 is the established, widely-used Taiwan Chinese name for Hermès (unlike 蔻馳/Coach). Spoken Mandarin reading dominates; some affluent speakers say Hermès, hence medium.
Established Chinese name; Taiwanese always say 麥當勞, never 'McDonald's'.
Taiwanese professor with native Chinese name; read in Mandarin.
Taiwan university dept; spoken naturally as the Chinese 交大資工.
Lab is inherently English/acronym-named; surface is already Latin and read as 'AIMEX Lab'.
Taiwan news media use the established Chinese name 華盛頓郵報; full Chinese surface is read in Mandarin.
Taiwan says 'Google' in English; 谷歌 is the Mainland name not used in Taiwan speech.
Surface already Latin; Taiwanese say 'Pinterest', no Chinese form used.
Guidance lists 保時捷=Porsche as code-switch TRUE; though 保時捷 is also common in Taiwan, English 'Porsche' is frequent for the marque.
Taiwan media use the Chinese transliteration 舒馬克 for the F1 driver.
Luxury car marque often referred to by English 'Aston Martin' in Taiwan; transliteration 奧斯頓馬丁 is bookish. Genuine judgment call.
WHO chief became famous in Taiwan during COVID as 譚德塞; Chinese transliteration is the standard spoken form.
Full Chinese surface 世界衛生組織 is read in Mandarin; though WHO acronym exists, the written Chinese name is read as Chinese.
Taiwan media use the Chinese transliteration 珍·古德 for Jane Goodall.
Broadcom CEO has a native Chinese name; Taiwan media say 陳福陽, not 'Hock Tan'.
輝瑞 is the dominant Taiwan name for Pfizer (heavily used during COVID, e.g. 輝瑞疫苗); some say Pfizer, hence medium.

Niche B2B semiconductor firm; the transliteration 美高森美 is rarely spoken in Taiwan's industry, where engineers code-switch to the English name. Name/data ambiguity (Microsemi vs Microchip) lowers confidence.
Taiwan airline; spoken naturally as 立榮航空.
Taiwan Foxconn-affiliated firm; spoken Chinese name 鴻準.
Taiwan company; the corporate name 合勤 is read in Chinese (brand Zyxel aside, surface is the Chinese name).
Taiwanese host with native Chinese name; read in Mandarin.
Korean actor; Taiwanese read 玄彬 in Mandarin, not 'Hyun Bin'.
Korean director; Taiwanese read 奉俊昊 in Mandarin, not 'Bong Joon-ho'.
Mainstream Taiwan media use the Chinese transliteration 彼得·提爾; tech circles may say 'Peter Thiel', so medium, but default Chinese.
Taiwan IC-design firm; spoken Chinese name 力旺.
Taiwan panel maker; spoken Chinese name 彩晶.
Taiwan government ministry; read in Chinese as 經濟部.
Taiwan company (ATEN brand); the corporate surface 宏正 is read in Chinese.
Taiwan textile/fiber company; Taiwanese read the Chinese name 宏益.
Western software brand; 歐特克 is a bookish transliteration nobody uses — Taiwanese say Autodesk. Explicit guidance example.
Honeywell is a well-known US industrial brand engineers say in English; 霍尼韋爾 transliteration is bookish. 自動化 descriptor may stay Chinese in speech, hence medium.
Obscure US healthcare-distribution firm; no common Taiwan Chinese usage and 麥肯森 is a bookish transliteration, so professionals would say McKesson — but low salience makes this a judgment call.
Taiwanese politician; always referred to by Chinese name 林佳龍.
Foreign airline with a fully established Taiwan Chinese name 新加坡航空 / 新航; Taiwanese say the Chinese, not 'Singapore Airlines'.
Surface is already Latin; Taiwanese read it as 'NTT DoCoMo' in Latin letters.
Taiwan securities firm; Taiwanese say 凱基證券.
Korean telecom; surface 'SK電訊' already carries Latin 'SK', and 電訊=Telecom, so 'SK Telecom' is a plausible spoken form, but dominant Taiwan usage is mixed — flag for review.
Swiss private bank; 瑞士寶盛 / 寶盛 is used in Taiwan financial media, though finance professionals may code-switch to 'Julius Baer' — genuine judgment call.
Western SaaS brand written in Latin; Taiwanese say Workday.
US industrial-automation brand known in Taiwan industry as 'Emerson'; 艾默生 is a bookish transliteration. Medium due to B2B salience.
Generic-pharma firm; 邁蘭 is a bookish transliteration and pharma professionals say 'Mylan', but low public salience makes it a judgment call.
Taiwanese researcher; Chinese name 林宏昀 is read in Mandarin.
Taiwanese researcher; Chinese name 呂昊謙 is read in Mandarin.
Western developer platform written in Latin; Taiwanese say GitHub.
Taiwan e-commerce brand spoken as 'momo購物網' (momo in Latin, 購物網 in Chinese); the full English 'momo.com' is not the spoken form, so keep the surface — TTS reads 'momo' in Latin anyway.
Western luxury brand; 蔻馳 is a transliteration nobody uses — Taiwanese say Coach. Explicit guidance example.
Japanese FMCG company; Taiwanese read 花王 in Mandarin, not 'Kao'. Explicit guidance example.
US retailer; 百思買 is a transliteration nobody uses — Taiwanese say Best Buy. Explicit guidance example.
Montblanc luxury brand; 萬寶龍 is an established and commonly used Taiwan Chinese name (unlike 蔻馳), though code-switching to 'Montblanc' also occurs — judgment call.
Western sportswear brand; 耐吉 transliteration nobody uses — Taiwanese say Nike. Explicit guidance example.
Chinese tech founder with native Chinese name; Taiwanese say 馬雲, not 'Jack Ma'. Explicit guidance example.
Western enterprise-software brand read as letters S-A-P; 思愛普 transliteration nobody uses. Explicit guidance example.
Western SaaS brand written in Latin; Taiwanese say Slack.
Taiwan YouTuber channel name 美式賣場 (Chinese); read in Mandarin, no Latin form (en empty).
Spoken as 'SOGO百貨' (SOGO in Latin letters, 百貨 in Chinese); the full English 'Pacific SOGO' is not the spoken form, so keep the surface — TTS reads 'SOGO' in Latin anyway.
Nivea skincare; 妮維雅 is the official, advertised, widely-used Taiwan Chinese name, though some speakers code-switch to 'Nivea' — judgment call.
Taiwanese actor; natively Chinese name, always spoken as 藍正龍, never 'Blue Lan'.
Taiwanese rock/pop singer; Chinese name is the only spoken form in Taiwan.
Taiwanese conglomerate; Taiwanese say 台塑/台塑集團, not 'Formosa Plastics'.
Taiwanese businessman (Formosa Plastics); native Chinese name, spoken in Mandarin.
Taiwanese tire maker; the company name 正新 is spoken in Chinese (its brand Maxxis differs, but the firm is 正新).
Taiwan-listed agribusiness; Taiwanese say 卜蜂, not the romanized Charoen Pokphand.
Alcoa is little known in Taiwan; the descriptive Chinese name 美國鋁業 is readable and the standard form, so a speaker would read the Chinese.
摩根大通 is the established Taiwan media name and dominant spoken form; finance insiders may say 'JPMorgan' but general usage reads the Chinese.
Obscure Western finance figure; Taiwan media use the Chinese transliteration, not 'Lloyd Blankfein'.
聯準會 is the universal Taiwan term for the US Federal Reserve; nobody code-switches to 'the Fed' in Taiwan spoken Mandarin.
Genuine judgment call: 三星 is very common in Taiwan spoken Mandarin, but Samsung is also widely used; leaning Chinese as dominant, flagged for review.
Taiwanese universally say 'AMD'; 超微 is a written form nobody pronounces aloud.
Western swimmer; Taiwan media use the Chinese transliteration 菲爾普斯.
Brazilian footballer; Taiwan uses the Chinese transliteration 內馬爾.
English stage name; the transliteration 巴德·邦尼 is not established, and Taiwanese listeners say 'Bad Bunny' in English.
K-pop group with an English stage name; surface is already Latin and Taiwanese say 'Red Velvet'.
路透/路透社 is the established and dominant spoken form in Taiwan news; some say 'Reuters' but the Chinese prevails.
Russian foreign minister; Taiwan media use the Chinese transliteration 拉夫羅夫.
Obscure Russian oil firm; 盧克石油 is the standard Chinese name and a speaker would read it rather than say 'Lukoil'.
Very obscure in Taiwan; the long descriptive Chinese name would be read aloud; almost nobody knows 'Engie'.
Pop star; 蕾哈娜 is a well-established Taiwan transliteration and the dominant spoken form, though some say 'Rihanna'.
Korean group; Taiwanese read the Chinese name 少女時代, not 'Girls' Generation'.
Taiwan media say 黃仁勳, not 'Jensen Huang'; natively Chinese name.
Taiwanese passive-components maker; spoken as 奇力新, not 'Chilisin'.
Taiwanese petrochemical firm; Taiwanese say 台塑化, not 'Formosa Petrochemical'.
Sun Life has low presence in Taiwan; 永明金融 is its official Chinese name and would be read aloud.
Western sports brand Taiwanese normally say in English ('Adidas'); per Taiwan code-switch norm for Nike/Adidas.
Decathlon is marketed and spoken in Taiwan as 迪卡儂; the Chinese name is the dominant, well-established spoken form.
